page_content:
AGNR200 AGNR 2+2 Program Extended Orientation Seminar (1 Credit) Introduces AGNR 2+2 students to University and "American" life. Assists with their successful transition to the College and Agriculture and Natural Resources and the University of Maryland. Helps students to understand their place in AGNR at to learn about the resources available to them. Prerequisite: Must be a ﬁrst semester international student enrolled in the AGNR 2+2 Program. Restriction: Must be a ﬁrst year international student enrolled in the College of Agriculture and Natural Resources 2+2 Program. Additional Information: It is strongly recommended that AGNR 2+2 students take AGNR 200 during the ﬁrst semester at the University. One class meeting per week. This courses is initiated by International Programs and will be coordinated in conjunction with the College of AGNR.

AAST443 Asian American Politics (3 Credits) Students will gain a greater understanding of 1) the role of Asian Americans in US politics, 2) the political attitudes and behaviors of Asian Americans and 3) how to conduct research on Asian American politics. Though the class will concentrate on Asian Americans, issues related to Asian American politics will be examined within the larger context of America's multicultural political landscape. Cross-listed with: AMST498J, GVPT368C. Credit Only Granted for: AAST498T, AAST443, GVPT368C or AMST 498J. Formerly: AAST498T.
